content:
- Read current Ralph summaries and the list-active-tasks skill.
- Current active summary shows one failing task in story-36 for verify-container Helm/raw Kubernetes manifests.
- Next: refresh task summary, inspect chooser rules and task definition, then write the selected task path if still unblocked.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries and verified the chooser rules against the active task definition.
- Confirmed the repository is a Nix-managed Rust workspace with a Makefile, and the only active failing candidate is the story-36 verify-container Kubernetes manifests task.
- Next: write that task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Re-read the refreshed active task summary and verified there is exactly one unblocked failing task.
- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt to point at the story-36 verify-container Kubernetes manifests task.
- Next context should execute that task, including manual Kubernetes verification and the required make check/make lint validation.

- Read previous progress log, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is story-36 verify-container Kubernetes manifests; next step is to inspect the task file and detect whether it is still TO BE VERIFIED or already NOW EXECUTE.
- If the task is still planning-state, create or update the plan file under the task's plans directory and stop immediately as required.

- Read the active story-36 task file and confirmed it is a manifest-only/manual-verification task with an explicit TDD exception.
- Did not find an obvious companion plan yet from the initial directory listing; next I am checking the end of the task file and searching for TO BE VERIFIED / NOW EXECUTE markers before creating a new plan.
- If there is no existing execution marker, I will create the required plan under the task's plans directory and stop immediately.

- Confirmed there is no existing plan or NOW EXECUTE marker for the active story-36 task, so this turn must end with a new plan file in the task-specific _plans directory.
- Before drafting the plan, I am reading a couple of existing plan files for structure and scanning the repo for verify-container / deployment surfaces to anchor the plan to the real current contract.
- After that I will write the plan, record its path, and stop immediately.

- Sampled the current verify-service public contract and compose surface; the service uses a config file, listener TLS paths, `/metrics` for health, and no Kubernetes deploy surface exists yet.
- The main boundary opportunity is to replace any duplicated raw-vs-Helm config rendering with one canonical manifest data model and keep only templating differences in Helm.
- Final planning step in this turn is to inspect how task files record plan paths, then write the new plan and task pointer before stopping.

- Created the planning artifact for story-36 at .ralph/tasks/story-36-verify-container-k8s-manifests/01-task-add-helm-and-raw-k8s-manifests-for-verify-container_plans/2026-04-29-verify-container-k8s-manifests-plan.md.
- Updated the task file to include a <plan> pointer to that plan. The plan ends in TO BE VERIFIED and covers the real verify-service runtime contract, the improve-code-boundaries cleanup target, the Nix-first local cluster decision gate, and the independent raw-vs-Helm manual verification slices.
- Did not execute the task. Next context should review the plan, switch it to NOW EXECUTE only when satisfied with the design, and then stop immediately per the task instructions.

- Read previous progress, confirmed the active task path, and reloaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- I am now reading the task file and its referenced plan to determine whether this context should stay in planning mode or execute the task.
- If the plan still ends in TO BE VERIFIED, I will only refine the plan/task metadata as needed and quit immediately per Ralph rules.

- Read the active story-36 task file and its referenced plan, then reviewed the plan against the actual verify-service operator guide, Compose example, and OpenAPI contract.
- The sampled sources confirmed the plan’s core execution assumptions: long-running verify-service listener, `/config/verify-service.yml` plus `/config/certs/*` mount contract, and `/metrics` or TCP connect as the honest health surface.
- I updated the plan terminator from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE at .ralph/tasks/story-36-verify-container-k8s-manifests/01-task-add-helm-and-raw-k8s-manifests-for-verify-container_plans/2026-04-29-verify-container-k8s-manifests-plan.md. Next context must execute that plan exactly, and if the real runtime contract diverges during execution it must switch the plan back to TO BE VERIFIED and stop immediately.

- Reloaded prior progress, the active task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the current task already has an execution plan advanced to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn must implement rather than re-plan.
- Next I am reading the task file and referenced plan, then inspecting the current Kubernetes/Helm surface before editing.

- Read the active task file and its referenced execution plan.
- Confirmed this is a manifest/manual-verification task with a TDD exception, so execution should proceed in small honest verification slices rather than by adding brittle tests.
- Next I am sampling the existing verify-service runtime/deploy contract and identifying one boundary cleanup that will keep raw manifests and Helm semantically aligned.

- Sampled the authoritative verify-service docs, OpenAPI, and compose example.
- Confirmed the Kubernetes contract should preserve `/config/verify-service.yml`, `/config/certs/*`, the `verify-service run --config ...` command shape, and `/metrics` or TCP as the health surface.
- Next I am checking image/build/deploy files, existing deployment directories, and local cluster/tooling availability before writing manifests.

- Confirmed there is no existing Kubernetes deploy surface in the repo.
- Tooling check shows `docker` and `nix` are present, while `kind`, `kubectl`, and `helm` are not preinstalled.
- Next I will try the Nix-provided cluster/tooling path first, while also inspecting image/build details and local verification prerequisites like how to provide real external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL endpoints.

- Probed the Nix-based tooling path first as required. `kind` and `kubectl` work from `nix shell`; the only issue was that `nixpkgs#helm` is the wrong package, so the next probe will use the Kubernetes Helm package.
- This means the Nix path is viable without broad flake surgery, so I do not need a non-Nix cluster fallback at this point.
- Next I am pulling TLS/database setup details from the repo’s existing e2e harness to keep the local external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL verification faithful to current repo assumptions.

- Confirmed the Nix path fully works with `nixpkgs#kind`, `nixpkgs#kubectl`, and `nixpkgs#kubernetes-helm`.
- Sampled the repo’s e2e harness and verify-service docs to anchor the local verification environment on real Cockroach/Postgres TLS generation patterns and the actual verify API (`POST /jobs`, `GET /jobs/{id}`).
- Next I am finalizing the deployment layout and then editing in one pass: raw kustomize surface, Helm chart, concise README, and any helper script needed for honest manual verification.

- Added the first full Kubernetes deployment surface under `deploy/kubernetes/verify-container/`: raw kustomize manifests, a matching Helm chart, the operator README, and a local verification preparation script.
- The main improve-code-boundaries cleanup is a single canonical `/config` contract shared across both surfaces, especially one projected `/config/certs` volume instead of fragmented secret mounts.
- Next I am validating the surface by rendering Helm and kustomize, fixing any structural mistakes, then moving on to image build/load and local cluster verification.

- Verified the new surface structurally: the preparation script passes `bash -n`, `helm template` renders, and `kubectl kustomize` renders once local input files exist.
- Next I am moving into the honest runtime verification slices: kind cluster creation, local verify image build/load, cert-manager install, and then external DB preparation plus raw/Helm installs.
- Any divergence found now will be handled in code rather than papered over in the docs.

- Static render checks are done and the real runtime setup is underway.
- `kind create cluster --name verify-container` and `nix build .#verify-image` are both running now.
- The next likely risk points are Docker image load/tag handling and pod connectivity to the external Dockerized databases over the kind network.

- `nix build .#verify-image` completed, producing `/nix/store/64dh1vjjzkvr64p6w0wgsvl2c50arhhc-docker-image-verify-image.tar.gz`.
- Loaded the image into Docker as `verify-image:0.1.4` and retagged it to `verify-image:k8s-local` for the local verification overlay.
- The cluster is still coming up; next is `kind load docker-image`, cert-manager install, external DB preparation, and then raw manifest application.

- Loaded `verify-image:k8s-local` into the kind node and installed cert-manager from its latest release manifest.
- cert-manager rolled out successfully; the remaining setup blocker is the local external DB preparation script, which I am polling because a silent hang there would invalidate the verification path.
- Next is either fixing that script/runtime setup or, if it succeeds, applying the raw overlay and checking real workload readiness.

- cert-manager is healthy, but the first real runtime failure appeared in the external DB setup: `verify-k8s-crdb` exited while `verify-k8s-postgres` stayed up.
- This is exactly the kind of fail-first signal the plan called for, so I am diagnosing the Cockroach startup failure before applying any manifests.
- Next I am reading the Cockroach container logs, adjusting the local verification helper if needed, and rerunning the setup slice.

- Diagnosed the first real runtime failure in the local verification helper.
- Fixed two real issues in `prepare-local-verification.sh`: secure Cockroach startup now uses `cockroach start` with distinct RPC/SQL ports, and both Cockroach/Postgres readiness loops now fail loudly instead of silently continuing after timeout.
- Next I am rerunning the helper from a clean container state and only proceeding to raw apply if the external databases come up honestly.

- Found a second real runtime mistake in the helper: Cockroach cluster init was targeting the SQL port instead of the RPC/listen port.
- Patched the helper so it now retries `cockroach init` on `26357`, then separately waits for the SQL endpoint on `26257` before seeding data.
- Next I am rerunning the local DB prep from a clean state again; only after that succeeds do the raw and Helm verification slices make sense.

- The latest helper failure was a PostgreSQL setup bug: `\gexec` inside `psql -c` does not work in this scripting path.
- Replaced it with an explicit shell-level database existence check and `CREATE DATABASE` only when needed.
- Next I am rerunning the helper again; if it completes, I finally have the real external endpoints and generated manifest inputs required for raw apply.

- The helper progressed past DB startup and seeding; the next failure was a script path bug, with generated files being written relative to `deploy/kubernetes` instead of `deploy/kubernetes/verify-container`.
- Corrected the helper root path so raw config, secrets, the local kustomize overlay, and Helm values land in the intended directories.
- Next I am rerunning the helper again from a clean state and then moving directly into raw apply if it completes.

- The local verification helper finally completed successfully.
- Real external endpoints now exist on the kind network, and the generated raw config/secrets plus Helm values file are in place for the same CockroachDB/PostgreSQL pair.
- Next is the raw-manifest tracer bullet: apply the raw overlay, wait for certificate and deployment readiness, and use the first runtime signal from the actual pod.

- The first raw apply failed because the generated local kustomize overlay lived under the raw base tree, which caused a parent-child cycle during accumulation.
- Moved the generated overlay path to `deploy/kubernetes/verify-container/.local-verification/raw-overlay/` and updated the README plus helper accordingly.
- Next I am regenerating the local inputs and retrying the raw apply immediately.

- The relocated overlay still failed because the generated `resources:` path was stale.
- Fixed the helper so the local raw overlay points at `../../raw` instead of its parent directory.
- Next I am regenerating the overlay one more time and retrying raw apply; this should finally shift failures from overlay plumbing to the actual workload.

- Raw apply succeeded: namespace, cert-manager issuers/certificates, secrets, service, and deployment all reached the expected ready state.
- The verify pod is running with the generated config and external DB endpoints.
- Next I am proving behavior rather than just readiness by port-forwarding the service, hitting `/metrics` over TLS with the issued CA, and then running a real verify job against the external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL pair.

- Raw verification passed the meaningful behavior gate.
- I successfully hit `/metrics` over the cert-manager-issued TLS chain and ran a real verify job to completion against the external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL pair; the result was `succeeded` with `rows_checked: 2` for `app.accounts`.
- Next I am deleting the raw install and repeating the same proof independently through the Helm chart.

- Raw install was cleaned up successfully.
- The first independent Helm attempt failed before any workload resources came up because the generated `values.local.yaml` is malformed YAML.
- Next I am inspecting the generated values file, fixing the block-scalar formatting bug in the helper, regenerating, and retrying the Helm install.

- Inspected the generated Helm values file and confirmed the PEM block contents were aligned with the YAML keys instead of nested under them.
- Fixed the helper to indent generated PEM contents correctly beneath the block-scalar keys.
- Next I am regenerating `values.local.yaml`, retrying the Helm install, and then repeating the same `/metrics` plus verify-job proof as the raw surface.

- The independent Helm install succeeded: the chart deployed, cert-manager issued the listener certificate, and the verify pod reached Ready.
- Next I am repeating the exact same behavior proof as the raw surface: port-forward the Helm-managed service, hit `/metrics` over the issued CA, and run a real verify job against the same external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL pair.

- Both deployment surfaces now passed the same end-to-end proof against the same external CockroachDB/PostgreSQL pair.
- Raw and Helm each served `/metrics` over the cert-manager-issued TLS chain and each completed a real verify job with `rows_checked: 2` for `app.accounts`.
- Next I am running `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, then I will update the task metadata only if all repo gates stay green.

- Both manifest surfaces are behavior-verified; the current phase is repo-wide validation.
- `make check` is running and rebuilding the main Nix Rust derivations.
- Any failures now are likely integration-quality issues around the wider repo gates, not uncertainty about the Kubernetes deployment contract.

- `make check` has progressed through build, clippy, and fmt, and is now in the nextest execution phase.
- I am waiting for the actual test run to finish rather than assuming green from the earlier build steps.
- If the repo gates stay clean, the next steps are `make lint`, `make test`, task file updates, and the Ralph task-switch/commit flow.

- `make check` completed through build, lint, format, and nextest with 88 tests passed and 19 skipped.
- `make lint` also returned cleanly from the cached Nix lane.
- `make test` is the last repo gate still running; if it passes, I can update task metadata and complete the Ralph task-switch / commit flow.

- Updated the task file to `completed` with `<passes>true</passes>`, checked acceptance boxes, and recorded the exact Nix, kind, cert-manager, raw, Helm, and repo-gate evidence.
- No AGENTS.md update looks necessary; the task-specific surprises are captured in the task notes and helper script instead of global instructions.
- Next is the final Ralph/git sequence: task switch script, git review, add/commit all files, and push.
